{{Quote|Nackt, Arfit, hit him from the left. I'll come from the right. All at the same time, you hear?|Sion directs Nackt and Arfit to attack Luke Skywalker|<ref name="FACPOV" />}}
'''Sion''' was a [[human]] [[Gender|male]] who worked for [[Jabba's criminal empire]]. Prior to joining [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure|Jabba's]] forces, Sion was a speeder thief. In [[6 BBY]],<ref name="Date">"Kickback" takes place during the [[Rescue of Han Solo]], which ''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'' dates to [[4 ABY]]. Sion is said to have worked for the [[Hutt Clan|Hutt Cartel]] for ten years, meaning he began working for them in [[6 BBY]].</ref> after he stole a prized [[podracer]] from [[Black Sun]], he was caught and was moments away from being executed until he was saved by [[Errin]], who worked for the [[Hutt Clan|Hutt Cartel]]. Sion joined his crew and worked alongside [[Nackt]] and [[Arfit]]. He eventually married [[Sigs]] and looked after a [[Sion's daughter|daughter]] and a [[Sion's son|son]].<ref name="FACPOV" />

The crew was on security detail when Jabba sentenced the [[Jedi Knight]] [[Luke Skywalker]] and the [[smuggler]]s [[Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca]] to be executed in the [[Great Pit of Carkoon]]. When the prisoners tried to escape, Sion led the charge to try and apprehend Skywalker. However, he was [[Death|killed]] when Skywalker kicked him into the [[sarlacc]] pit. His last thoughts were of his family.<ref name="FACPOV" />

{{Char-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
In the story "[[Kickback (short story)|Kickback]]" from the book ''[[From a Certain Point of View: Return of the Jedi]]'', which is told from Sion's point of view, he is surprised by a kick in the chest from Luke Skywalker, who is seemingly too far away.<ref name="FACPOV" /> This reveals that Sion is the character who, in the movie [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|Return of the Jedi]], is shown reacting to a kick that does not connect.<ref name="Episode VI" /> Fans have dubbed this the "Force-kick" to explain what was likely a camera angle error. [[Mark Hamill]] joked about the scene on Twitter in 2023, calling the move his "widely celebrated, perfectly executed Force-kick."<ref name="Tweet">{{Twitter|MarkHamill|status/1662529702207303680|Mark Hamill|date=27-05-2023|quote=How could anyone possibly be bothered by my widely celebrated, perfectly executed Force-Kick?! {{C|In response to: [A GIF of Luke force kicking Sion]}}|archivedate=20230527215830}}</ref>
